news.headline: Merrett praises Hird, not drawn on future after Scott sacking
news.body: Former Bombers captain Zach Merrett has a strong relationship with James Hird, but wouldn't be drawn on Brad Scott's replacement.
news.ai_summary: Zach Merrett remains contracted at Essendon through 2026 and the club expects him to honour that deal, but ongoing coaching turmoil — Brad Scott's sacking makes it nine senior coaches in his career — keeps trade speculation alive and adds risk to his long-term Fantasy appeal. There's no immediate impact on his role or job security as the Bombers' midfield engine, so his scoring ceiling and price trajectory are unchanged in the short term. Coaches holding Merrett can stick with him as a premium mid, but monitor the off-field noise and any tactical shift under interim coach Dean Solomon that could affect his midfield time or kick-in/run-with duties.

news.headline: BARRETT: Obsession with past greats set to shape Bombers' coach call
news.body: Will Essendon go back to its 'red and black security blanket' in the form of James Hird? It would be the most Essendon thing ever, writes Damian Barrett.

news.headline: Bombers land exciting Tasmania speedster with No.1 pick
news.body: Essendon has taken Jaxon Artemis with the No.1 pick in the Mid-Season Rookie Draft.
news.ai_summary: Essendon snared rebounding half-back Jaxon Artemis with pick No.1 in the Mid-Season Rookie Draft, and with 14 Bombers sidelined for the West Coast trip, he's a strong chance to debut immediately under caretaker Dean Solomon. At rookie price with damaging ball-use and run from defence, he shapes as a cash-cow option for fantasy coaches if locked into the Round 13 side. Richmond's Kye Annand (DEF) and West Coast's Oliver Francou (MID) are the other notable fantasy-relevant selections, with Annand filling Gibcus's hole and Francou offering inside-mid upside. Monitor team news late in the week before pulling the trigger.

news.headline: AFL responds to claims of interchange breach in Pies-Hawks draw
news.body: The League says there was no breach of the interchange rules in the final seconds of Collingwood's draw with Hawthorn in round eight.
news.ai_summary: No fantasy implications here — the AFL has cleared Hawthorn of any interchange breach in the Round 8 draw with Collingwood, with no result change, no suspensions and no sanctions handed down. Jack Gunston, James Sicily and Dylan Moore are all unaffected from a selection, role or pricing standpoint. Coaches can ignore this one and move on.

Monitor official team sheets later in the week before locking in round 12 moves.

news.headline: The full list of nominees for the 2026 Mid-Season Rookie Draft
news.body: Check out the full list of players who have nominated for the AFL Mid-Season Rookie Draft.
news.ai_summary: Likely No.1 pick Jaxon Artemis (Tasmania) headlines a 429-strong nomination list for Tuesday's Mid-Season Rookie Draft, with Essendon holding first selection and Collingwood, Port Adelaide and Melbourne each able to take up to three players. Geelong VFL's Kye Annand and North Melbourne VFL midfielder Xavier Bamert are also drawing strong interest, while AFL-experienced names like Jaidyn Stephenson, Nathan Kreuger and James Bell offer potential cheap cash-cow options if drafted into senior roles. With only up to 19 picks available, coaches should monitor landing spots closely — rookie-priced MSD draftees who slot straight into Round 11 sides are prime DPP/job-security targets. Watch for role clarity post-draft before committing trades.

news.ai_summary: The Bulldogs edged Melbourne in a nail-biter to bank a crucial win. Check the box scores for fantasy-relevant output from key Dogs and Dees.

news.headline: Nine things we learned: Leave this young Dockers star loose at your peril
news.body: The Giants' season is still alive, Richmond has found a bargain-basement recruit plus more lessons from round 11.
news.ai_summary: Don't sleep on Fremantle's emerging young gun—he's burning opponents when left unattended and shaping as a must-watch fantasy option. Round 11 also flagged GWS keeping their finals hopes alive and Richmond unearthing a cut-price recruit worth a look.

news.headline: Giant killers! Historic blitz sees GWS demolish reigning premiers
news.body: A 14-goal third-quarter has set Greater Western Sydney up for a shellacking of Brisbane.
news.ai_summary: GWS unleashed a record-breaking 14-goal third quarter to humiliate reigning premiers Brisbane. Expect huge fantasy scores from Giants midfielders and forwards, while Lions owners brace for ugly numbers across the board.

news.headline: Dogs, Dees lock in final teams for important clash. Follow LIVE
news.body: Follow all the action from Sunday's round 11 games.
news.ai_summary: Bulldogs and Demons have confirmed final teams for Sunday's crucial Round 11 clash — check your lockouts and finalise trades now. Live coverage rolling across all Sunday games.

news.headline: Giants create AFL history with onslaught, Dogs down Dees in classic
news.body: Greater Western Sydney has left reigning premier Brisbane reeling with a record-breaking goal surge, while the Bulldogs get home in a see-sawing contest against the Demons.
news.ai_summary: GWS unleashed a historic goal blitz to smash reigning premier Brisbane — Lions owners should brace for ugly scores, while Giants attackers are must-watch. The Bulldogs edged Melbourne in a thriller, keeping their high-scoring mids firmly in fantasy calculations.

news.headline: Dockers roll on with stars set to return, Lyon on 'bizarre' Wilkie treatment
news.body: Justin Longmuir's side is well placed to tackle the reigning premier at the Gabba in round 12.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle are humming and could welcome key stars back for a tough Gabba clash with Brisbane in Round 12 — monitor returning Dockers for potential POD value. Meanwhile, Lyon's "bizarre" handling of Wilkie raises a selection cloud worth watching before lockout.

news.headline: Simpkin's powerful No.36 tribute inspires stirring performance
news.body: After a tumultuous off-season also marred by tragedy, Jy Simpkin is adding to his lofty reputation this year.
news.ai_summary: Simpkin is in career-best form, channelling emotion into elite output and shaping as a must-consider midfield option. Coaches chasing a value-priced Roo gun should have him firmly on the radar.

news.headline: How Giants are driving cultural progress with new First Nations leaders
news.body: GWS has made two significant off-field appointments to help battle the decline in First Nations talent.
news.ai_summary: GWS has appointed two new First Nations leaders off-field to address the league-wide decline in Indigenous talent. No direct fantasy impact, but signals the Giants' long-term investment in player development pathways.

news.headline: The Lions are limping, but this is why you shouldn't write them off
news.body: The back-to-back premiers aren't in flag form but their best could be just around the corner.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane are scratchy but far from done — keep faith with your Lions premiums, as a form spike looks imminent.

news.headline: 'It's fun, winning': Fraser says Blues finding belief, Carr ready to 'reset' flickering Power
news.body: Successive wins has Carlton feeling good about itself, while Yartapuulti heads to its bye in need of a break.
news.ai_summary: Carlton's two-game winning streak is restoring confidence and could spark a fantasy revival for underperforming Blues; meanwhile, Port Adelaide limps into its bye, making their players risky holds until form returns.

news.headline: Why Pies' record-breaker isn't done yet, and the 'crazy' moment that made him uncomfortable
news.body: After breaking the all-time games record, Pies champion Scott Pendlebury isn't ready to stop yet.
news.ai_summary: Scott Pendlebury has no plans to retire after breaking the all-time AFL games record, signalling the Pies champ remains a viable midfield-forward option for fantasy coaches chasing durable, cash-cow-proof veterans.

news.headline: New Blues go back-to-back with big win over Power
news.body: Josh Fraser has extended his early perfect record in charge at Carlton with a strong win over Yartapuulti.
news.ai_summary: Carlton are 2-0 under new coach Josh Fraser after dispatching Port Adelaide, with the Blues' midfield rolling and looking like a fertile hunting ground for fantasy points.

news.body: Check out your club's biggest ever comeback win.
news.ai_summary: North Melbourne nearly broke their club record for biggest comeback in a stunning win over the Suns — check where your club's greatest comeback ranks.

news.headline: Roo-diculous! Zurhaar sinks Suns after siren to secure epic comeback win
news.body: Cam Zurhaar nails the shot after the siren to give North Melbourne a six-point win.
news.ai_summary: Zurhaar's after-the-siren goal seals a stunning Roos comeback, boosting his fantasy relevance as a forward option in form. Gold Coast owners should be wary of late-game collapses denting their players' scoring upside.

news.headline: Bombers learn severity of injuries after brutal Dreamtime outing
news.body: The Bombers have confirmed the severity of several of the injuries they suffered on Friday night.
news.ai_summary: Trade out alert: Andrew McGrath (fractured jaw) and Matt Guelfi (hamstring tear) are both out until after Essendon's Round 15 bye, making them immediate trade targets before price drops hit. Sam Durham is in concussion protocols and will miss at least Round 12 vs West Coast — hold if you can stomach one week, but monitor closely. Jye Caldwell is under an injury cloud with an ankle/syndesmosis issue, adding further risk to Bomber owners. Archie May has been cleared and should play, offering some rare good news for cheapie holders.

news.headline: Brent Harvey is about to lose his place in AFL history
news.body: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.
news.ai_summary: Scott Pendlebury overtakes Brent Harvey today as the VFL/AFL games record holder, a largely ceremonial milestone with minimal fantasy impact given Pendlebury's well-established role and pricing. Harvey tips North Melbourne's Harry Sheezel as a future contender, citing his durability (just one game missed since being drafted in 2022) — a strong tick for fantasy coaches considering the premium mid as a long-term hold. No injury or role changes flagged for either player.

news.headline: F1 driver smashes into groundhog during Canadian Grand Prix session
news.body: A groundhog is killed and an F1 car heavily damaged after a collision during practice for the Canadian Grand Prix.
news.ai_summary: This article is about Formula 1, not AFL, so there are no fantasy implications for SuperCoach or AFL Fantasy coaches. No selection, injury, role, form or price points can be drawn for AFL purposes. Coaches should disregard for fantasy considerations.

news.headline: The five-hour drive that started Sun's long road to an AFL spot
news.body: Oscar Adams is establishing himself as a regular for Gold Coast's best 23 but it wouldn't have happened without his mum's long hours behind the wheel.
news.ai_summary: Oscar Adams has cemented himself in Gold Coast's best 23, playing seven games in 2026 as a key defender alongside Sam Collins and Mac Andrew, taking the spot vacated by the dropped Charlie Ballard. His role as an intercepting "come-forward defender" is locked in for now, with Ballard out of favour reducing job-security risk. Coming off arguably his best game against Port in Darwin, he's a deep-league DPP defender option for keeper leagues rather than a short-term cash cow, given his low base price and improving output. Monitor his scoring trend before pulling the trigger in Fantasy/SuperCoach.

news.headline: Hawks star makes return as AFL comeback draws nearer
news.body: Will Day makes his return from injury in the VFL on Friday night.
news.ai_summary: Will Day is firmly off fantasy radars as a starting option, but his successful VFL return (16 disposals, 6 tackles, 2 clearances in managed minutes) puts an AFL comeback on the horizon. With Sam Mitchell confirming a senior recall is purely medical-dependent and not performance-based, Day could return within the next fortnight. Given his elite midfield ceiling when fit, he's a name to monitor as a potential POD trade target once he strings games together — though injury risk and role/minutes management make him a wait-and-see play rather than an immediate buy.

news.headline: Peter Wright under an injury cloud
news.body: Peter Wright is in doubt after a knee complaint.

news.headline: Watch: Dayne Zorko
news.body: Whispers Dayne Zorko is racing the clock on a soreness ahead of the weekend.

news.headline: Blues claim spirited victory over Dogs to begin post-Voss life in style
news.body: Carlton beats the Western Bulldogs by 12 points in its first game since Michael Voss left the club, while Melbourne upsets Hawthorn in style. Follow live.
news.ai_summary: Carlton ended a seven-game losing streak with a 12-point upset over the Bulldogs under interim coach Josh Fraser, with Patrick Cripps (29 disposals, 2 goals) dominating the final term — a strong hold for SuperCoach owners despite his price. Matt Carroll (3 goals) and debutant Jack Ison (15 touches, sealing goal) emerge as cheap rookie/mid-price watch-list options, while Harry McKay backed up last week with two goals. Melbourne's midfield kept rolling against Hawthorn: Jack Steele (26, 6 clearances), Tom Sparrow (27, 8) and Max Gawn (18, 8 clearances) remain premium holds, with Harvey Langford (2 goals) and Harrison Petty (3 goals) building forward-line value. Hawks owners should note Jack Gunston (out) and Tom Barrass (out) missed, while Nick Watson was tagged by Tholstrup — Dylan Moore (27, 2 goals) was their lone reliable scorer.

news.headline: Mannagh's heavenly night leads Cats to statement win over Lions
news.body: Geelong records a 41-point win over Brisbane on the back of Shaun Mannagh's five-goal, 30-disposal performance at the Gabba.
news.ai_summary: Shaun Mannagh is the headline act for fantasy coaches after a career-best 30 disposals and 5 goals against Brisbane, suggesting a price rise and renewed trade appeal as a mid-priced forward. Bailey Smith was elite again (34 touches, 11 tackles, 7 clearances, 2 goals) and remains a must-hold premium mid. Tom Stewart (17 intercepts) reminded owners of his defensive ceiling, while Darcy Wilmot (31) continues to justify his D1 role for Brisbane. On the downside, Dayne Zorko was a late withdrawal (calf), Hugh McCluggage had a rare poor outing, and Lachie Neale was tagged by Oisin Mullin — monitor before locking Lions mids in.

news.headline: Reaction Time: Your club's Urgency Index after round 11
news.body: Riley Beveridge takes a look at the hot topic at your club this week.
